model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Round of 16 · 5 July 2026 · East Rutherford. Final score: Brazil 1–2 Norway. Erling Haaland dragged Norway to their first-ever World Cup quarter-final and sent five-time winners Brazil crashing out.

Norway had never been this far; Brazil had never gone home this early since 1990. History swung on a single, breathless finish.

The goals, in sequence: 79′ Erling Haaland — 0–1; 90′ Erling Haaland — 0–2; 100′ Neymar (pen) — 1–2. That is 3 goals in all, Brazil accounting for 1 and Norway 2.

Goalkeeper Ørjan Nyland had already produced a sensational display, saving a first-half penalty from Bruno Guimarães to keep the tie level. Then Haaland took over, striking twice in the final eleven minutes — his sixth and seventh goals of the tournament — to stun the favourites. Neymar's stoppage-time penalty was little more than a consolation for a Brazil side that had run out of ideas. For a Norwegian generation long told this could not be done, Haaland was busy proving himself wrong.

Momentum, as ever in the knockouts, proved a fragile and fleeting thing.

For the record, Norway advanced. Goal, team and minute for every event above are Merkle-proven on Solana devnet via TxLINE; the scorer names are attributed from public match reports and aligned to the verified goals by team and order. Nothing here rests on the commentator's word alone.
